# Contributing

Please use feature branches, follow Conventional Commits, update documentation, and open a Pull Request.
# Contributing

Thank you for your interest in contributing to GitHub Readme Stats Deployment.

This project aims to provide a production-ready deployment template for self-hosting GitHub Readme Stats using Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx, and GitHub Actions.

---

## Before You Start

Please:

- Search existing issues before opening a new one.
- Read the project documentation.
- Follow the repository coding and documentation standards.

---

## Development Workflow

1. Fork the repository.
2. Create a feature branch.

   feature/<short-description>

3. Make focused, well-documented changes.
4. Update documentation when applicable.
5. Submit a Pull Request.

---

## Commit Messages

This project follows Conventional Commits.

Examples:

feat(docker): add multi-stage Dockerfile

fix(compose): correct volume mapping

docs(readme): update deployment guide

build(ci): add GitHub Actions workflow

---

## Pull Requests

Before submitting a Pull Request, ensure:

- Documentation is updated.
- The deployment process has been tested.
- New files follow the existing project structure.
- Changes are focused and atomic.

---

## Code Style

- Keep configuration readable.
- Prefer simplicity.
- Avoid unnecessary complexity.
- Document architectural decisions using ADRs when appropriate.

---

Thank you for contributing.
